commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a TypeBox description to the message tool `channel` parameter and a regression assertion for that generated schema text.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level issue: current main exposes `channel` as an undescribed optional string in the generated message tool schema, and the linked report explains the resulting `Unknown channel` failure. I did not run a live Slack or Discord workspace repro.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed for contributor real behavior proof and for deciding whether this description-only PR should supersede the richer draft enum PR.

target.title: fix(message): describe channel parameter as provider name
target.body:
Summary
- Clarify the message tool `channel` parameter as a messaging provider name, such as `slack` or `discord`.
- Add coverage that the generated schema describes `channel` as a provider name rather than a conversation/channel ID.

Fixes #10354

Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`

Behavior addressed: The `message` tool schema now explains that `channel` is the provider name, not a channel or conversation ID.
Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout, Node v24.13.1, pnpm v11.1.0.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: `2 passed (2)` test files and `118 passed (118)` tests.
Observed result after fix: The schema description assertion passes with the updated `channel` description.
What was not tested: Full repository test suite.
target.author: zhengzuo0-ai
